title:

rawContent:
Tweet by @NoahEpstein_ (Nozz)
Author bio: Automating the boring stuff and sharing the blueprint || F$£K around and find out.
Text: i've been the complete opposite

openclaw out the box? Not great, I can’t lie, but that’s with most things. 

but that's the point. it's not a finished product you just use. it's infrastructure you build on.

i've done a full backend overhaul:

- 62 agents across 3 companies (personal brand, SaaS, and the agency)

- persistent memory per chat. every discord channel, every telegram thread remembers what we talked about last week (because what usually goes down is every time you start a new chat it just loads MEMORY, SOUL and AGENT and that’s it)  

- each company has its own workspace with its own context, its own memory, its own agent hierarchy

- LLM picker that routes tasks to the right model. not everything needs opus. some stuff runs on flash for free 

- and one workspace specifically dedicated to openclaw improving itself. it reviews its own memory and architecture daily

the memory problem people complain about? that's a config problem. once you wire up channel memory + workspace memory + event logs, it genuinely knows what's going on across sessions.

it's not a chatbot. it's an operating system. but like any OS, it's only as good as how you set it up, and build on it.
